observation_descriptions.description: The market experiences a significant and rapid price decline over a short period, indicating panic selling.
Trading volume is significantly higher than the average, suggesting heightened trading activity and potential panic selling.
The daily price range (high-low) is unusually wide, reflecting increased volatility and market instability.
A noticeable decrease in daily trading volume after a period of high volume, indicating a potential reduction in selling pressure and market stabilization.

formulas.name: formula001
formulas.description: Measures the percentage decline in the closing price over a 5-day period, indicating sharp price declines associated with panic selling.

formulas.name: formula002
formulas.description: Standardized score of the closing price decline over the past 5 days, highlighting significant deviations from the mean as evidence of panic selling.

formulas.name: formula003
formulas.description: Five-day percentage change in closing price, capturing rapid declines linked to panic selling.

formulas.name: formula004
formulas.description: Standardized score of volume over 5 days, indicating unusually high trading activity that may reflect panic selling.

formulas.name: formula005
formulas.description: Ratio of the 5-day moving average of volume to the 20-day moving average, showing increased trading activity during suspected panic selling periods.

formulas.name: formula006
formulas.description: Five-day percentage change in volume, capturing spikes in trading activity associated with panic selling.

formulas.name: formula007
formulas.description: Standard deviation of the daily price range over 5 days, reflecting increased volatility and market instability.

formulas.name: formula008
formulas.description: Normalized range between the maximum high and minimum low over 5 days, indicating wider price ranges during volatile market conditions.

formulas.name: formula009
formulas.description: Standardized score of the daily price range over 5 days, identifying unusually wide ranges indicative of market instability.

formulas.name: formula010
formulas.description: Negative percentage change in volume over 5 days, capturing decreases in trading activity after initial high volumes, suggesting reduced selling pressure.

formulas.name: formula011_v2
formulas.description: Average decrease in volume over 5 days compared to the previous 5-day period, indicating a reduction in trading activity following high volume periods.

formulas.name: formula012
formulas.description: Negative standardized score of volume over 5 days, suggesting decreases in trading activity after high volume periods.

observation_descriptions.description: A recent session has experienced a significant increase in volume, indicating a panic sell-off event.
There is a noticeable sharp decline in closing prices over the recent sessions, reflecting the panic sell-off.
Following the sharp decline, trading sessions show a reduced intraday range, indicating a decrease in volatility and potential exhaustion of selling pressure.
Volume is declining after the initial spike, reflecting a withdrawal of panicked sellers and less aggressive selling activity.

formulas.name: formula001
formulas.description: This formula measures the Z-score of volume over the past 5 sessions. A higher value indicates an unusual spike in volume, suggesting a potential panic sell-off.

formulas.name: formula002
formulas.description: This formula captures the relative change in volume compared to the previous session. A higher value suggests a significant increase in volume, consistent with a panic sell-off.

formulas.name: formula003
formulas.description: This formula computes the ratio of the 3-day moving average of volume to the 20-day moving average. A higher ratio indicates a recent spike in volume relative to the longer-term average, suggesting panic selling.

formulas.name: formula004
formulas.description: This formula calculates the three-day price change relative to the current closing price. A more negative value indicates a sharper decline, reflecting panic sell-off behavior.

formulas.name: formula005
formulas.description: This formula measures the Z-score of closing prices over the past 5 sessions. A lower value suggests that the current close is significantly below its recent average, indicative of a sharp decline.

formulas.name: formula006
formulas.description: This formula calculates the ratio of the minimum closing price over the past 5 days to the 20-day average closing price. A lower ratio indicates a sharper decline, suggesting panic selling.

formulas.name: formula007
formulas.description: This formula computes the ratio of the 5-day average of the intraday range to the 20-day average. A lower value suggests a reduction in volatility and potential selling exhaustion.

formulas.name: formula008
formulas.description: This formula measures the standard deviation of the intraday range over the past 5 days. A lower value indicates reduced volatility following a decline.

formulas.name: formula009
formulas.description: This formula calculates the 5-day average of the absolute difference between the closing and opening prices. A lower value suggests a decrease in intraday range, reflecting reduced volatility after a sell-off.

formulas.name: formula010
formulas.description: This formula calculates the change in the 5-day moving average of volume over the past 3 days. A negative value indicates a decline in volume, suggesting a withdrawal of panicked sellers.

formulas.name: formula011
formulas.description: This formula measures the Z-score of volume over the past 10 sessions. A lower value indicates a decline in volume post-spike, reflecting reduced selling activity.

formulas.name: formula012
formulas.description: This formula computes the ratio of the 5-day moving average of volume to the 10-day moving average. A lower ratio suggests a decline in volume after an initial spike.

observation_descriptions.description: The market experiences a significant increase in volume, indicating heightened trading activity commonly associated with a sell-off.
There is a substantial decline in the closing price, reflecting a significant sell-off.
The daily high-low price range begins to narrow, suggesting a reduction in volatility and selling pressure.
Volume begins to decrease from previously heightened levels, indicating a reduction in trading activity and potentially less selling pressure.

formulas.name: formula001
formulas.description: Measures the z-score of the volume over 7 days. A higher value indicates an increase in volume compared to the recent mean, suggesting heightened trading activity.

formulas.name: formula002
formulas.description: Calculates the percentage change in volume from the previous day. A higher percentage indicates a significant increase in trading activity.

formulas.name: formula003
formulas.description: Ranks the volume in the past 7 days. A higher rank indicates that the current volume is among the highest in the recent period, suggesting increased trading activity.

formulas.name: formula004
formulas.description: Measures the negative z-score of the closing price over 7 days. A higher negative z-score indicates a larger decline in price from the recent mean.

formulas.name: formula005
formulas.description: Calculates the negative percentage change in the closing price from the previous day. A higher value indicates a larger decline, reflecting a significant sell-off.

formulas.name: formula006
formulas.description: Ranks the closing price in the past 7 days. A higher rank in the negative indicates a significant decline in the closing price.

formulas.name: formula007
formulas.description: Measures the standard deviation of the high-low range over 7 days. A lower standard deviation suggests a narrowing price range.

formulas.name: formula008
formulas.description: Calculates the mean of the high-low range over 7 days. A lower mean indicates a narrowing of the price range.

formulas.name: formula009
formulas.description: Ranks the high-low range in the past 7 days. A lower rank indicates a narrowing range.

formulas.name: formula010
formulas.description: Measures the negative z-score of the volume over 7 days. A higher negative z-score suggests a decrease in trading activity from previously heightened levels.

formulas.name: formula011
formulas.description: Calculates the negative percentage change in volume from the previous day. A higher value indicates a larger decrease in trading activity.

formulas.name: formula012
formulas.description: Ranks the volume in the past 7 days. A higher rank in the negative indicates a decrease in volume, suggesting reduced trading activity.

observation_descriptions.description: The market has experienced a prolonged and significant decline in price over several days, indicating extended panic-driven selling.
There is a notable surge in trading volume, suggesting heightened activity often associated with panic selling.
A reduction in daily price range, implying a decrease in volatility which may indicate a pause in aggressive selling pressure.
An increase in closing prices from intra-day lows, suggesting early signs of buying interest as prices recover slightly by the end of the trading day.

formulas.name: formula001
formulas.description: Measures the cumulative price decline over the past 9 days. A more negative value indicates a more extensive decline, suggesting panic-driven selling.

formulas.name: formula002
formulas.description: Average Z-score of closing prices over the past 9 days. Lower values suggest significant declines, indicating extended panic selling.

formulas.name: formula003
formulas.description: Minimum daily percentage change in closing prices over the past 9 days. Lower values indicate more significant declines, characteristic of panic selling.

formulas.name: formula004
formulas.description: Z-score of trading volume over the past 9 days. Higher values suggest a surge in volume, often associated with panic selling.

formulas.name: formula005
formulas.description: Average daily percentage change in trading volume over the past 9 days. Higher values indicate a surge in activity, suggesting panic selling.

formulas.name: formula006
formulas.description: Maximum daily percentage change in trading volume over the past 9 days, representing peak activity levels that may indicate panic selling.

formulas.name: formula007
formulas.description: Average daily price range over the past 9 days. A reduction in this range suggests decreased volatility and possibly a pause in aggressive selling.

formulas.name: formula008
formulas.description: Z-score of daily price ranges over the past 9 days. Lower values indicate a reduction in volatility, suggesting a pause in aggressive selling.

formulas.name: formula009
formulas.description: Minimum daily price range over the past 9 days. Lower values suggest reduced volatility, indicating a pause in aggressive selling pressure.

formulas.name: formula010
formulas.description: Average recovery from intra-day lows to closing prices over the past 9 days. Higher values suggest early signs of buying interest as prices recover by the end of the trading day.

formulas.name: formula011
formulas.description: Z-score of the difference between closing prices and intra-day lows over the past 9 days. Higher values suggest a recovery from lows, indicating potential buying interest.

formulas.name: formula012
formulas.description: Maximum relative recovery from intra-day lows within the daily range over the past 9 days. Higher values indicate potential buying interest as prices close higher relative to intra-day lows.

validation_errors: formulas[13] (name='formula014') definition must be continuous numeric (no comparisons/logical ops). Found operators: <. Definition: -(COUNT(low < DELAY(low, 1), 10) / 10)
